channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Russia unleashes largest aerial assault on Ukraine, pummeling Kyiv with hundreds of drones
item.description: Russia launched the biggest aerial attack on Ukraine over a two-day period since the war began, striking Kyiv and multiple cities with hundreds of drones in a devastating barrage that killed at least two and wounded dozens.

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Lebanon ahead of US-mediated ceasefire talks
item.description: Israeli airstrikes hit cars on a highway south of Beirut, killing 12 people, as Lebanon and Israel prepare for a third round of Washington-mediated talks with their current ceasefire set to expire within days.

item.title: Iran war devastates Middle East economies and upends regional security
item.description: The US-Israeli bombing campaign against Iran has damaged infrastructure across the region, exposed Gulf energy vulnerabilities, and overturned decades of assumptions about Middle East security architecture.

item.title: NATO eastern flank leaders call for consolidated air defenses after Russian violations
item.description: Leaders of 14 NATO allies on the eastern flank warned that repeated Russian airspace breaches underscore the urgent need to strengthen the alliance's air defense capabilities against missiles and drones.

item.title: Xi Warns Trump That Taiwan Could Push US-China Relations Into "Dangerous Place"
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping issued a stark warning to President Trump during their Beijing summit, saying the Taiwan issue could push bilateral relations into dangerous territory if mishandled.

item.title: Iran Says Ready to Repel New US Attack as Peace Talks Stall
item.description: Iran's military said it is trained and ready for any new US assault as President Trump predicted a "long talk" about the war with China's President Xi in Beijing.

item.title: Philippines seeks ICC suspect after Senate shootout chaos
item.description: Philippine authorities are hunting for Senator Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa wanted by the ICC for drug war crimes after a chaotic confrontation at the Senate.

item.title: South Korea showcases Cheongung-II missile defense and KF-21 fighter in military demonstration
item.description: South Korea's military offered a rare look at its flagship defense programs, demonstrating the Cheongung-II missile defense system and KF-21 fighter jet amid rising regional tensions.

item.title: Russia places UK ex-defense minister Ben Wallace on wanted list
item.description: Russia has placed former British Defence Minister Ben Wallace on its wanted list, a move seen as retaliation for UK support of Ukraine.

item.title: Heavy Russian strikes kill one, wound 31 in Kyiv
item.description: Russia pummeled Kyiv and other regions in a massive aerial attack that killed one person and wounded 31, continuing the largest drone assault of the war.

item.title: M23 Rebels and Rwandan Soldiers Accused of War Crimes in DR Congo
item.description: Human Rights Watch documented summary executions, rape, and forced recruitment during M23's offensive in eastern DR Congo, with Rwandan forces directly participating.

item.title: Quarter of World Cup Games at Risk of Dangerous Heat, Scientists Warn
item.description: Climate change has increased the risk of extreme heat at the 2026 World Cup, with about a quarter of matches facing potentially dangerous conditions for players and fans.

item.title: Iran war overshadows Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
item.description: Donald Trump is expected to ask China to help end the costly and unpopular Iran war in discussions with Xi Jinping, with peace talks stalled and global economic costs mounting.

item.title: Gold rises as markets watch Trump-Xi talks and Iran war
item.description: Gold prices ticked up on Thursday, supported by a weaker dollar, as investors focused on the Beijing summit and ongoing Iran conflict for direction.

item.title: Oil markets look to Xi-Trump summit for Iran war relief
item.description: The increasingly stressed crude oil market is looking to the Xi-Trump summit for any signs of de-escalation in the Iran conflict that has disrupted global energy flows.

item.title: Chinese yuan hits 3-year high ahead of Trump-Xi summit
item.description: China's yuan edged up to a three-year high against the dollar, while stock indexes dipped as the high-stakes Trump-Xi summit began in Beijing.

item.title: US House members force vote on Ukraine aid, defying leadership
item.description: A U.S. House petition to force a floor vote on providing security aid to Ukraine has gathered enough signatures to bypass leadership and bring the measure to the floor.

item.title: Iran War Casts Shadow Over BRICS Foreign Ministers Meeting in Delhi
item.description: The US-Israeli war on Iran is testing BRICS unity as foreign ministers gather in New Delhi for talks, with the conflict exposing divisions within the bloc.

item.title: Trump-Xi Summit Begins in Beijing with Trade and Iran War on the Agenda
item.description: President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping opened high-stakes talks in Beijing with trade negotiations and the Iran war dominating the agenda.

item.title: Russia Oil Output Drops 460,000 Barrels Per Day Amid Ukraine Strikes
item.description: Russian crude production fell to 8.8 million barrels per day in April, down 460,000 bpd from a year earlier as Ukrainian attacks on energy infrastructure and sanctions take their toll.

item.title: Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair Amid Rising Inflation Pressures
item.description: The US Senate confirmed Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair by a 54-45 vote, the most contentious confirmation for a Fed chair in decades, as inflation concerns mount.

item.title: China stages naval drills near Philippines as US launches Balikatan exercises
item.description: China's PLA Southern Theatre Command conducted naval exercises east of Luzon, coinciding with US-Philippines joint military drills in the region.

item.title: Germany opens trial of "Ulm 5" activists over Elbit arms firm attack
item.description: Five activists face trial in Stuttgart for a September 2025 attack on an Israeli-owned Elbit Systems facility in Ulm, amid rising scrutiny of Israel-Germany defense ties.

item.title: Chinese AI satire of US-Iran war goes viral on state media
item.description: A wuxia-style animated allegory by CCTV depicting the "White Eagle" vs "Persian Cat" conflict has gone viral on Chinese social media, framing the US-Iran war through historical analogy.

item.title: Iran explores yuan-for-Hormuz oil trade plan, Chinese analysts urge caution
item.description: Reports suggest Iran may allow oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if traders pay in yuan, but Chinese analysts warn such a system would face major practical and sanctions hurdles.
